codeReview.summary: Commit 18136f2 resolves the two declared core-fidelity audit gaps (disabled intake CTA visual and removal of the top BackLink on group-mismatch) and retains required retry CTA routing/error-state content for the reviewed rotate-keyset core screens. No blocking or non-blocking code defects were found in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-builder skill contains guidance that conflicts with mission conventions: it says not to use Tailwind utility classes, while mission AGENTS explicitly requires exact Tailwind-style token classes when quoted by the validation contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: web-demo-v2/.factory/skills/ui-builder/SKILL.md:91 ('Do NOT use Tailwind utility classes') vs mission AGENTS.md:49 (exception requiring quoted classes like bg-[#EF44441A]); reviewed commit uses class token bg-[#2563EB40] in src/screens/RotateKeysetScreens.tsx for VAL-RTK-001.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-builder skill procedure says to invoke the agent-browser skill, but actual worker execution used direct agent-browser CLI commands via Execute; this mismatch should be clarified to reduce procedural ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: web-demo-v2/.factory/skills/ui-builder/SKILL.md:33 and :128 instruct 'Invoke agent-browser skill'; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId cdeba91d-2c33-42d1-bf87-5abeb2deab85 shows Execute commands beginning with 'agent-browser --session ...' and no Skill call for agent-browser.

summary: Pass: rotate-keyset-core-fidelity implementation is acceptable with no code issues found in commit 18136f2; two documentation/procedure alignment observations were recorded for shared state.
